since_last: 46% of 87 AI names green; Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads +0.70%; AI bubble risk 49 Neutral.

cards.headline: The tape: a mixed, rotational tape, 46% of AI names green
cards.body: SPX +0.30% · BTC -2.75% · Gold +1.05% · Nasdaq100 +0.21%. 46% of 87 AI names are green — a mixed, rotational tape. Chip Equipment & AI Hardware is doing the heavy lifting at +0.70%.

cards.headline: Chip Equipment & AI Hardware leads, AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech lags
cards.body: Strongest AI sector is Chip Equipment & AI Hardware at +0.70%; weakest is AI Platforms, Internet & AdTech at -2.06%. Rotation within the AI trade, not a one-way move.

cards.headline: MU is the standout at +9.62%
cards.body: Leaders: MU +9.62%, AMAT +5.85%, ROK +5.00%, TER +4.63%, CGNX +4.39%. Laggards: DELL -7.49%, AAPL -4.98%, PLTR -4.30%, AI -4.24%.
cards.watch: Hot means attention, not direction — watch whether the leaders hold the bid or fade into the next session.

cards.headline: SpaceX (SPCX) -1.34% at $152.48
cards.body: The SpaceX debut keeps pulling attention — SPCX is -1.34% this session. The biggest IPO in history remains the tape's attention magnet.

cards.headline: Risk: AI bubble index 49 — Neutral
cards.body: The composite AI Bubble Index sits at 49/100, led by Valuation at 85. Higher = frothier; the risk is everyone caring at once while valuations are already stretched.
